semantic.role: This document defines the automated release pipeline for the ArchSpine project, triggered by version tags.
responsibilities: Specifies the workflow that runs on pushes to tags matching 'v*'
Checks out the repository, sets up Node.js 20, installs dependencies and builds
Publishes the package to the npm registry using the NPM_TOKEN secret
Creates a GitHub Release with auto-generated release notes
outOfScope: Pre-release validation or changelog generation beyond GitHub notes
Multi-platform builds or deployment to other registries

English.purpose: This document describes the release workflow of the ArchSpine project, an automated pipeline that builds, publishes to npm, and creates a GitHub release whenever a version tag is pushed.
English.context_and_audience: It is intended for maintainers and contributors who need to understand how official releases are cut, as well as for developers integrating ArchSpine via npm who want assurance about the release automation.
key_takeaways: The release is triggered by any tag starting with 'v' (e.g., v1.2.3).
The workflow runs on Ubuntu, uses Node 20, and caches npm dependencies.
After building, it publishes to npm using a secret token and creates a GitHub Release with auto-generated notes.
